key_insight: The paper turns a numeric table into a statistical “fingerprint” so different datasets can be compared, retrieved, and aligned even when they do not share the same variables.
engineering_relevance: This enables safer dataset search, transfer, and initialization for materials and simulation workflows when raw data or consistent feature names are unavailable.
method: Compute exploratory-data-analysis descriptors for each numeric dataset.
Embed descriptors into a shared vector space with a pretrained sentence transformer.
Use CCA, plus a sparse penalized variant, to measure similarity and recover interpretable cross-dataset variable correspondences.
evidence_signal: Across 15 datasets, the method achieved a total P@1 of 0.9, with nearest-neighbor retrieval and clustering remaining robust under embedding ablations and differential-privacy settings.
watch_item: Interpretability depends on the quality and stability of the engineered descriptors, and differential privacy may trade off utility against alignment fidelity.
paper_title: Statistical Embeddings for Similarity, Retrieval, and Interpretable Alignment of Numeric Tabular Datasets
